q: The Doppler effect is a change in frequency of a wave related to an observer.
Doppler effect is caused by the relative motion between the source and observer.
The Doppler shift is a change in the wave's speed.
Christian Doppler was an Austrian physicist.
Christian Doppler described the phenomenon of Doppler effect in 1842.
Only the motion of the source and observer directly towards or away from each other matters in the reference frame.
Change of pitch heard when a vehicle sounding a horn approaches is an example of Doppler shift.
The general form of the Doppler equation is $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$.
In the Doppler equation, $f_s$ represents the frequency heard by the observer.
In the Doppler equation, $f_o$ represents the frequency heard by the observer.
The variable $v$ in the Doppler equation represents the speed of sound in the medium.
The variable $v_o$ in the Doppler equation represents the speed of the source.
The variable $v_s$ in the Doppler equation represents the speed of the source.
Appropriate signs must be taken into account when using the Doppler equation.
When source and observer approach each other, $f_o > f_s$.
When source and observer recede from each other, $f_o > f_s$.
When source and observer recede from each other, $f_o < f_s$.
$+v_o$ is used when the observer moves towards the source.
$-v_o$ is used when the observer moves towards the source.
$-v_o$ is used when the observer moves away from the source.
$-v_s$ is used when the source moves towards the observer.
$+v_s$ is used when the source moves towards the observer.
$+v_s$ is used when the source moves away from the observer.
When observer and source are stationary, $v_o = v_s = 0$.
Doppler effect is observed only in sound waves.
Doppler effect is observed in electromagnetic radiations.
Doppler effect can be observed in radio waves and microwaves.
Red shift is a manifestation of the Doppler effect in light from a star.
In red shift, wavelength shifts toward the red end of the spectrum.
Red shift corresponds to a higher frequency and shorter wavelength.
Red shift corresponds to a lower frequency and longer wavelength.
Red shift occurs when earth and a star move away from each other.
If a star approaches earth, spectral lines shift towards the blue end.
Radar stands for radio detection and ranging.
Radar is a form of echolocation that uses sound waves.
Radar uses electromagnetic waves.
The Doppler effect is used in radar to measure the velocity of detected objects.
Police radar and air traffic control radar use the Doppler effect.
Weather radar is an application of the Doppler effect.
Doppler ultrasound is used to examine blood flow.
Blood flow rates can be found by measuring the Doppler shift in ultrasound reflected by red blood cells.
Angiodynography is the technique measuring Doppler shift in ultrasound reflected by red blood cells.
Doppler ultrasound can help reveal blockage to blood flow.
Formation of plaque in arteries can be revealed by Doppler ultrasound.
Doppler ultrasound provides information on the heartbeat of a fetus during labor.
Echocardiography is the technique used to examine the heart.
Doppler ultrasound cannot be used to examine kidneys.
If source and observer move in the same direction with same velocity, there is no Doppler shift.
